source_jobs.title: General Interest - Future Roles
source_jobs.description:
Don’t see a current opening that matches what you’re looking for? We frequently open new roles!



While this form isn’t linked to a specific job, it lets us learn a little more about you in the meantime. We'll reach out if there’s a need that matches your skills and experience.


We're excited to meet you!

source_jobs.title: Developer Relations - Go to Market
source_jobs.description:
Show developers how Chromatic/Storybook solves challenges in frontend delivery, and help our go-to-market (GTM) team convert that into revenue across both product-led and sales-led funnels.

You’ll join our team to create technical content and programs that resonate with developers and accelerate deals. We’re looking for someone who can translate product features into compelling narratives with measurable business impact.

This is a fully remote position, and the candidate should be located within the US, Canada, or Europe.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create developer-facing content that supports the lead pipeline like articles, guides, webinars, talks, videos, case studies, landing pages, and demos.

 - Partner with Marketing and Sales to embed developer voice in campaigns.

 - Run programs (e.g. webinars, workshops, live streams) that bring developers into the funnel and nurture them toward adoption.

 - Collect feedback from the field and distill developer pain points for GTM, Sales, and Product.

 - Maintain a high bar for technical accuracy and clarity to earn developer trust.

 - Proactively spot opportunities to equip GTM with developer narratives, rather than waiting for direction.


REQUIREMENTS

 - You’ve built real software and already held a developer marketing, advocacy, or equivalent role.

 - You’re humble, curious, and motivated by developer outcomes—not farming engagement.

 - 5+ years of experience building modern web UIs (JavaScript/TypeScript, React or similar).

 - Strong portfolio of technical communication: articles, talks, tutorials, or videos.

 - Excellent communication for both developer and business audiences.

 - Demonstrable success working with Marketing, Sales, and Customer Success on developer-facing campaigns.

 - Proven ability to build trust with developer communities.


YOU HAVE A BUSINESS MIND AND A DEVELOPER HEART

This role is part of the DX organization but embedded within GTM. You’ll work with DX engineers to ensure technical rigor and alignment with our company narrative. You’ll collaborate directly with Marketing and Sales on programs that expand adoption and accelerate pipeline. Your success is measured in GTM outcomes: influence on product-led and sales-led funnels, and closed revenue.


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

 - Dominic https://www.linkedin.com/in/domyen/ - CxO

 - Claire https://www.linkedin.com/in/clairereitz/ - Marketing

 - Varun https://twitter.com/winkerVSbecks - DX Engineer

 - Kyle https://twitter.com/kylegach - DX Engineer

Salary Range: US$155K - US$175K + equity. Learn more about our compensation philosophy here https://chromatic-ui.notion.site/Chromatic-s-Compensation-Philosophy-bc0b2c567f6e44ac8d2ada3003404a93. For a list of our other benefits & a peek into life at Chromatic, visit our careers page https://www.chromatic.com/company/careers.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Manager - Developer Marketing
source_jobs.description:
PRODUCT MARKETING MANAGER – DEVELOPER MARKETING

At Chromatic, our mission is simple: help teams build better, more trustworthy UI faster. Chromatic automatically tests and verifies UI changes across browsers, viewports, and themes, catching UI bugs before they hit production. We’re trusted by frontend teams at companies of all sizes, including GitHub, Adobe, BBC, Perplexity, and Shopify, to ensure UIs are tested and approved before reaching the billions of people who interact with them daily.

We’re hiring a Product Marketing Manager to create the technical assets that help engineering teams and leaders understand, adopt, and derive value from Chromatic’s and Storybook’s platforms. You’ll be on the Marketing team and will work directly with Sales, Developer Experience, and Product to support how developers learn, evaluate, and use our product.

This role is heavily execution-focused with an emphasis on building actionable, developer-centric content and assets that power marketing campaigns and sales enablement. Show developers and technical buyers how Chromatic and Storybook solves challenges in frontend delivery while directly impacting both product-led and sales-led funnels for the Go-to-Market team.

This is a fully remote position in the United States, Canada, or Europe.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Write deep technical blog posts, tutorials, and guides that help engineers understand and implement Chromatic features.

 - Build comparison pages, and case studies that help teams evaluate Chromatic against alternatives.

 - Develop copy and logic for interactive assets, such as quizzes and calculators, with deep technical expertise.
   
   - Bonus: Ability to build and implement the interactive landing pages you design

 - Build and implement onboarding and nurturing assets, such as chat flows and email drip campaigns, to increase product adoption.

 - Run live webinars and workshops that bring developers into the funnel and nurture them toward adoption.

 - Develop technical sales enablement assets, including demos, decks, and talk tracks that embed a developer voice into the sales process.

 - Collect feedback from the field and distill developer pain points for GTM, Sales, and Product.

 - Help develop product positioning and messaging for product launches and feature launches alongside Developer Experience.

 - Maintain a high bar for technical accuracy and clarity to earn developer trust.

 - Proactively spot opportunities to equip GTM with developer narratives, rather than waiting for direction.
   
   


REQUIREMENTS

 - 5+ years of product marketing experience for developers.

 - Deep understanding of the technical side of developer marketing and ability to speak competently to developers.

 - Demonstrable success in building multi-use assets that helped generate pipeline and product adoption.

 - Proven ability to build trust with developer communities with a point of view biased toward long-term meaningful engagement over shortcuts.
   
   


WE’D BE EXTRA EXCITED IF YOU…

 - Have experience in Product Marketing in frontend engineering tools or developer testing tools.

 - Experience in web development and ability to build pages (JavaScript/TypeScript, React or similar).

 - Have experience directly impacting product adoption and revenue pipeline at a startup.

 - Have worked effectively in a distributed, remote-first team environment.
   
   

Salary Range: $122 - 165k + equity. Learn more about our compensation philosophy here https://www.notion.so/chromatic-ui/Chromatic-s-Compensation-Philosophy-bc0b2c567f6e44ac8d2ada3003404a93. For a list of our other benefits & a peek into life at Chromatic, visit our careers page https://www.chromatic.com/company/careers.




WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

 - Claire https://www.linkedin.com/in/clairereitz/ - Marketing

 - Dominic https://www.linkedin.com/in/domyen/ - CxO

 - Jeff Lewis https://www.linkedin.com/in/jeff-lewis-3577046/- CRO

 - Varun https://x.com/winkerVSbecks - DX Engineer

source_jobs.title: Senior Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
At Chromatic, our mission is to improve the UX of the internet and safeguard the world's pixels. We're building tools that make it impossible to ship broken UI. Thousands of frontend teams—including BBC, Shopify, and Adobe—use Storybook and Chromatic to map every state of their applications and ensure each one is tested and approved before reaching the billions of people who interact with them daily.

We’re seeking a Product Manager to own company objectives for improving our platform. You’ll work directly with customers to understand their asks and drill down to the root causes for the problems they’re facing. You will also work closely with our design, engineering, and marketing teams to craft product enhancements in support of the metrics backing our objectives. You’ll own these features end-to-end from discovery to delivery and work with engineering teams across the platform to do it.

Over the past year, highlights from our team's work include:

 - Launched an accessibility regression testing product to help customers continuously improve the accessibility of their front-ends.

 - Improved efficiency of agentic workflows with the Storybook MCP hosted by Chromatic as well as other enhancements across the platform.

 - Built visual regression testing support for React Native Storybook.

We're at a significant moment in our company's history. We're running a successful business at tremendous scale and your work will immediately impact customers worldwide. We need your expertise to help take us to the next level. Shipping with momentum matters, but we also make space to think ideas through carefully and ship the right things. Our diverse team is full of passionate, curious people who can learn, grow, and feel supported in taking on new challenges. We dive into complex problems and create simple, high-quality, delightful solutions. If you're energized by building developer tooling and being an active user of the product you work on, this is the perfect job for you.

This is a fully remote position on a cross-functional team for individuals based in the mainland US or Canada.




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Own the vision for end-to-end business objectives that span multiple engineering teams.

 - Synthesize your ideas, our business goals, customer feedback, product analytics and insights gleaned from the marketing/sales teams into feature specifications.

 - Work closely with the engineering and marketing teams to ensure we ship world class software that matters to our existing and future customers.

 - Manage analytics instrumentation across our products and use it to make data driven product decisions.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years product management experience with SaaS companies.

 - Clear written and verbal communicator who provides straightforward, self-contained updates with necessary context in our distributed, asynchronous environment.

 - Technical background strong enough to understand how our tools are used by developers.

 - Strong opinions, weakly held—someone who pushes back on ideas to drive better outcomes, handles critique without defensiveness, and demonstrates a growth mindset.

 - Evidence-driven mindset—uses customer feedback and our analytics instrumentation to make informed product decisions.

 - Accountability—experience leading the end-to-end process for a feature from conception to release.


WE’D BE EXTRA EXCITED IF YOU…

 - Were previously a front-end software engineer.

 - Have experience working in the developer tooling space.

 - Have a keen interest in UI, design and the front-end ecosystem.

 - Have worked effectively in a distributed, remote-first team environment.

We understand that even the best candidates might not meet all the above qualifications on day one. That's okay. If you’re excited about Chromatic’s mission and want to grow your skills, we’d love to hear from you.



Salary Range: 170,000 - 210,000 USD + equity. Learn more about our compensation philosophy here https://www.notio

source_jobs.title: Sales Engineer (Pre-Sales)
source_jobs.description:
Chromatic is looking for a Sales Engineer to partner closely with our Sales team and drive the technical success of our Enterprise deals. In this role, you’ll lead the technical evaluation process, from first conversation through proof-of-concept, helping engineering teams understand how Chromatic and Storybook fit into their workflows and deliver measurable value.

You’ll act as a trusted technical advisor to prospective customers, guiding architecture decisions, solving complex frontend and CI challenges, and demonstrating how modern teams can adopt component-driven development at scale, while showcasing the importance and impact of visual regression testing in building reliable UIs.

This is a high-impact, revenue-critical role where your ability to connect technical depth with business value will directly influence pipeline progression, deal success, and company growth.



This is a fully remote position and the candidate must be located in Central or Eastern Time Zone within the US or Canada.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Partner with Account Executives to drive the technical win in Enterprise sales cycles

 - Translate product capabilities into clear business and engineering impact for stakeholders (from ICs to leadership)

 - Lead technical discovery to understand prospect architecture, workflows, and challenges

 - Deliver tailored product demos that clearly articulate Chromatic and Storybook’s value for frontend teams

 - Own and execute proof-of-concepts (POCs) and technical evaluations

 - Guide prospects through integrating Chromatic into their CI/CD pipelines and development workflows

 - Advise on best practices for scaling component-driven development and modern front-end testing across teams.

 - Address technical objections, including security, performance, and architecture concerns

 - Collaborate with prospects’ engineering teams to solve real-world implementation challenges during evaluations

 - Support RFPs, security reviews, and technical due diligence processes

 - Partner with Product and Engineering to relay customer feedback and shape roadmap priorities

 - Continuously improve demo environments, evaluation processes, and technical sales collateral

 - Stay current with frontend trends and emerging best practices, bringing thoughtful perspectives into conversations with Product and Engineering leaders


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

Experience:

 - 3+ years in a pre-sales technical role

 - Experience working at a B2B SaaS company

 - Strong frontend engineering background with experience in modern JavaScript frameworks (e.g., React, Webpack, Vite)

 - Familiarity with CI/CD pipelines and developer workflows (GitHub Actions, GitLab CI, etc.)

Technical Skills

 - Ability to quickly understand complex technical environments and propose practical solutions

 - Experience running demos, technical discovery, proof-of-values, and proof-of-concepts

 - Clear and confident communicator, able to translate complex technical concepts into compelling narratives that resonate with different audiences (engineers, managers, and executives)

Soft Skills:

 - Strong situational awareness and the ability to “read the room,” allowing you to quickly understand stakeholder dynamics, adapt your approach in real time, and build trust with both technical and non-technical audiences

 - High emotional intelligence and relationship-building skills; you know how to navigate complex customer organizations and create alignment across teams

 - A consultative mindset — you ask thoughtful questions, listen deeply, and tailor solutions to each customer’s unique context rather than jumping to generic answers

 - Strong sense of ownership and accountability, especially in high-stakes, deal-driven environments

 - Growth mindset — you’re curious, proactive about learning, and constantly looking to refine your craft (both technically and in how you engage with customers)

 - Enjoy navigating ambiguity and working in a fast-paced, evolving environm

source_jobs.title: Developer Experience Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Developer Experience sits at the intersection of engineering, education, and marketing. In this role, you’ll create demos, articles, docs, and technical content that help developers adopt new tools with confidence.

We’re looking for someone who can translate technical capability into clear, credible developer experiences that accelerates the business.



Required Location: North America or Europe




RESPONSIBILITIES

 - Create content like articles, guides, talks, videos, landing pages, and demos.

 - Develop integrations, plugins, and micro-sites.

 - Run programs like webinars and workshops

 - Partner with Marketing and Sales to embed developer voice in campaigns.

 - Collect feedback from the field and distill developer pain points for Product.

 - Maintain a high bar for technical knowledge to earn developer trust


REQUIREMENTS

 - You’ve built real software and held a developer experience, relations, or equivalent role.

 - Mission-oriented: You’re humble, curious, and motivated by delivering outcomes

 - Extensive front-of-frontend experience (e.g animations, tricky layouts)

 - Strong portfolio of technical communication: articles, talks, tutorials, or videos.

 - Excellent communication for both developer and business audiences.

 - Demonstrable success working with Marketing and Sales on developer-facing campaigns.

 - Proven ability to build trust with developer communities.


WHO YOU’LL WORK WITH

 - Dominic https://twitter.com/domyen - CxO

 - Claire https://www.linkedin.com/in/clairereitz/ - Marketing

 - Varun https://twitter.com/winkerVSbecks - DX Engineer

 - Kyle https://twitter.com/kylegach - DX Engineer

Salary Range: US$175K - US$194K + equity. Learn more about our compensation philosophy here https://chromatic-ui.notion.site/Chromatic-s-Compensation-Philosophy-bc0b2c567f6e44ac8d2ada3003404a93. For a list of our other benefits & a peek into life at Chromatic, visit our careers page https://www.chromatic.com/company/careers.

notes: Chromatic is a product-led company with a strong focus on developer experience and marketing.
